definitions.technical: The risk that a failure in traditional banking (such as a bank collapse) causes harm in stablecoin and DeFi markets. This is the direction of contagion that the comment letter argues the proposed rule does not adequately address.
definitions.lay: The risk that problems in the traditional banking system spread into the crypto world. The SVB collapse demonstrated this: a bank failure caused a stablecoin to lose its dollar peg, which then triggered automated selling cascades across DeFi platforms.
definitions.parenthetical: (the risk of contagion flowing from traditional banking failure into stablecoin and DeFi markets)
definitions.legal_significance: Reverse-channel risk is the comment letter's primary regulatory concern. While the GENIUS Act effectively addresses forward-channel risk (crypto to bank), its framework largely ignores the possibility that a traditional bank failure can destabilize stablecoin markets, as demonstrated by the SVB episode.
